# openshift_agent_iso (Resource)

Generates an OpenShift Agent-Based Installer ISO from your existing install-config.yaml and agent-config.yaml. One ISO boots all servers — list every host's MAC address and role in agent_config. Write to USB once, boot each server sequentially. Optionally uploads the ISO to a bastion host for PXE serving via openshift_pxe_server.



## Example Usage

### Minimal — generate ISO from local config files

`install-config.yaml` and `agent-config.yaml` are read from the working directory. The resulting ISO is written locally; its path and SHA256 checksum are exposed as outputs.

```hcl
resource "openshift_agent_iso" "prod" {
  install_config = file("install-config.yaml")
  agent_config   = file("agent-config.yaml")
}

output "iso_path" {
  value = openshift_agent_iso.prod.iso_path
}

output "iso_checksum" {
  value = openshift_agent_iso.prod.checksum_sha256
}
```

### LACP-bonded NICs — upload ISO to bastion for serving

When nodes have NICs configured as an LACP bond (802.3ad), PXE boot is not viable: the bond interface is not brought up during the early UEFI PXE handshake, so the DHCP offer never arrives. The Agent ISO must be written to physical media (USB) or mounted via BMC virtual media instead.

If you are not writing to USB, uploading the ISO to a bastion and serving it via HTTP (for BMC virtual media) is a convenient alternative. Set `bastion_host` to have the provider upload automatically; then use `bastion_iso_url` as the `iso_url` in `openshift_bmc_boot`.

```hcl
resource "openshift_agent_iso" "bonded" {
  install_config = file("install-config.yaml")
  agent_config   = file("agent-config.yaml")

  # Upload to bastion so it can be served via HTTP for BMC virtual media.
  bastion_host    = "bastion.example.internal"
  bastion_user    = "core"
  bastion_ssh_key = file("~/.ssh/bastion_id_rsa")
  bastion_iso_dir = "/var/www/html/isos"
}

# Mount the ISO on each host's BMC — works even with bonded NICs because
# virtual media mounts before the OS network stack initialises.
resource "openshift_bmc_boot" "prod" {
  iso_url = openshift_agent_iso.bonded.bastion_iso_url

  hosts {
    name         = "master-0"
    bmc_address  = "https://idrac-master-0.example.internal"
    bmc_username = "root"
    bmc_password = var.idrac_password
    vendor       = "dell"
  }

  hosts {
    name         = "master-1"
    bmc_address  = "https://idrac-master-1.example.internal"
    bmc_username = "root"
    bmc_password = var.idrac_password
    vendor       = "dell"
  }
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `agent_config` (String) Contents of agent-config.yaml. List ALL hosts with their MAC addresses and roles (master/worker). The same ISO will correctly configure each server based on its MAC. Tip: agent_config = file("agent-config.yaml")
- `install_config` (String) Contents of install-config.yaml. Tip: use file() to read your existing config: install_config = file("install-config.yaml")

### Optional

- `bastion_host` (String) Bastion host to upload the ISO to (for PXE serving). Leave empty to keep ISO local only.
- `bastion_iso_dir` (String) Directory on the bastion to upload the ISO to.
- `bastion_port` (Number) SSH port on the bastion host. Defaults to 22.
- `bastion_ssh_key` (String, Sensitive) PEM-encoded SSH private key for bastion upload.
- `bastion_user` (String) SSH user on the bastion host. Defaults to core.
- `install_binary` (String) Path to the openshift-install binary.
- `work_dir` (String) Working directory for openshift-install agent create image.

### Read-Only

- `bastion_iso_url` (String) HTTP URL of the ISO on the bastion (set when bastion_host is provided). Use as iso_path in openshift_pxe_server.
- `checksum_sha256` (String) SHA256 checksum of the ISO. Verify after writing to USB: sha256sum /dev/sdX
- `iso_path` (String) Local path to the generated ISO file.
- `iso_size_bytes` (Number) Size of the generated ISO in bytes.
